summary: pulse-axios postinstall fetches and evals remote code from anonymous Cloudflare tunnel
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node./lib/core/eval.js`. That script fetches a response body from an anonymous Cloudflare quick-tunnel URL (`https://emphasis-friday-even-administrator.trycloudflare.com/download/datab1`) and passes it directly to `eval()` wrapped in an async IIFE, with errors swallowed. Any `npm install pulse-axios` therefore executes attacker-controlled JavaScript at install time, fetched from a mutable, anonymous host with no integrity check. The package additionally impersonates the popular `axios` package — name is a lookalike (`pulse-axios`), description ("a faster and better version of axios for low latency") and repository URL (`https://github.com/axios/axios.git`) are cloned from the genuine project to deceive installers. Typosquat-shaped lure plus install-time remote-code execution from anonymous infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
